open_gaps.SEC-002: LOW (unchanged). Still no numPages>1000 cap at the viewer.js loadDocument() load boundary, and no cumulative-queued-bytes cap at the multi-file ingest boundaries (merge.js queued PDFs, img2pdf.js queued images). This tick's new module (flip-pages.js TASK-356) is NOT a new ingest boundary — it reads ONLY the ALREADY-OPEN, already-validated document via currentDoc.getData(), builds a FRESH pdf-lib doc (open doc never mutated), and uploads nothing. Pure pdf-lib structural imposition (embedPage + drawPage with a negative-scale mirror transform, no rasterization — low memory, safe for the 1.6 GiB box) and produces BOUNDED output: exactly numPages pages, same size/order as the source. Same LOW self-DoS family as the other per-page consumers (uploader's own tab only, no server impact), bounded by the same single fix — the viewer-load page-count cap. No new boundary, no separate cap needed beyond that. All remaining gaps are client-side-only DoS on the 1.6 GiB box; no server-side risk. Single right developer fix remains: page-count cap once at viewer load (covers all per-page consumers) + cumulative-bytes caps in merge.js/img2pdf.js addFiles().
